column61:
Identification (CE) : The customer reported that the Missed Call Alert service is active on his number. He explained that when he calls someone and the SIM is switched off, after the SIM returns to the network he does not receive any SMS notification indicating that the number has become available again.
The CM did not properly understand the customer’s question and, without following the correct modality, deactivated and then reactivated the customer’s Missed Call Alert service. This action was incorrect.

According to our rules, when the Missed Call Alert service is active on a number and the SIM is switched off, once the SIM returns to the network the SIM owner will receive an alert SMS in the following format:
“You were called by: +8801817182575 (2) on 07-Jun-2022 at 02:55 PM.”
The caller will not receive any SMS. Therefore, this call is considered a CE.

column64:
The customer called to unbar a lost phone/SIM. The agent collected the NID, Date of Birth, and MyGP app usage information correctly. However, when the customer failed to provide the last pack purchase details and asked for an alternative, the agent simply said there were no other options.

This is an incomplete verification process. According to the protocol, if a customer cannot provide a specific piece of information, the agent must attempt to cross-match any two of the following:

F&F (Can be omitted if not applicable to the package).

Last pack purchase.

Emergency Balance (EB) usage and amount.

MyGP usage.

Star Status and segment.

Since the customer was using a "Nischinto" package, the F&F question could be skipped, but the agent failed to ask about Emergency Balance as an alternative verification step. Because the agent did not exhaust all available verification options before denying the service, this is a fail.
